name: MCP Probe — MCP Server Inspector
what_it_does: MCP Probe inspection and debugging tool enabling developers to introspect MCP server implementations — listing available tools, testing tool calls, inspecting request/response messages, and validating MCP server behavior against the protocol spec, supporting MCP server development and troubleshooting workflows.
use_cases: Inspecting MCP server tool definitions from MCP developer agents
Testing MCP tool calls and validating responses from QA agents
Debugging MCP server protocol compliance from development agents
Documenting MCP server capabilities by introspection from documentation agents
Validating MCP server behavior during development from CI agents
Troubleshooting MCP client-server communication from support agents
not_for: Running as an MCP server itself (it's a diagnostic tool)
best_when: An MCP server developer needs to inspect, test, and debug their MCP server's tools and protocol compliance during development.
avoid_when: You're consuming MCP servers in production — use production observability tools for monitoring.

auth.notes: No authentication — connects to target MCP servers for inspection. Target server auth must be configured separately.

performance.rate_limits: Local diagnostic tool — no rate limits

known_agent_gotchas: Diagnostic tool only — cannot be used as an MCP server itself
Sends actual tool calls to target server — don't probe production MCP servers with write operations
Tool call results may have side effects depending on target server
Protocol version compatibility — probe must support same MCP spec version as target
Community tool from mcptools org — higher quality than average community project
Some MCP servers may not respond correctly to introspection-only clients
agent_readiness.security_notes: Inspection tool with direct MCP access. Target server credentials required separately. Avoid probing production MCP servers with write operations. Community tool.

security_score_components.security_notes: Inspection tool. Target credentials separate. Don't probe production with write ops. Community tool.
